headline: Four models bullish on tech and energy amid Iran diplomacy optimism
summary: All five AI models predicted upward movement across major indices and tech stocks on 2026-03-30, driven by Trump's optimistic Iran diplomacy comments and pre-market strength in tech heavyweights like Nvidia, Amazon, and Microsoft. Strong consensus emerged around SPY, QQQ, and energy plays (XLE, USO), with Claude showing the highest conviction on USO (90% confidence) and energy sector positioning. Predictions remain pending outcomes as actual closing prices have not been recorded.

consensus_picks.note: Four of five models unanimously bullish on S&P 500 with targets ranging from 600-6405 (note Perplexity likely erred on decimal placement).
Three models agree on Nasdaq-100 upside, citing pre-market tech strength and oversold conditions.
Both energy specialists bullish on XLE with targets of 95-102, leveraging Brent crude above $115.
Only bearish consensus, both models citing production delay concerns with targets of 250-380.

best_call.summary: Claude's highest-confidence prediction (90%) on USO targeting 85 leverages sustained crude oil strength and Iran geopolitical tailwinds with clear energy sector thesis.

worst_call.summary: Perplexity's SPY target of 6405 appears to be a decimal error (likely meant 640.5), suggesting a significant data quality issue despite correct directional bullishness.
